Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Asset Information Services - Senior Data & Analytics Engineer

Contract Length: 4 Months
Location: Burnaby, British Columbia




Raise is currently hiring an Asset Information Services - Senior Data & Analytics Engineer on behalf of our client. They’re expanding their team to meet growing needs, making this a unique opportunity to work with an industry leader. Our Client, is one of the largest electrical energy suppliers in Canada




Note: The primary pay rate is based on T4 classification; however, we will also consider applications from candidates interested in an INC classification, where applicable.




Description

The Senior Data & Analytics Engineer will play a critical role within the Asset Information Services (AIS) Analytics and Reporting team. This position is responsible for the design, development, and optimization of complex data architectures that support ’s asset performance and compliance reporting. You will lead the integration of disparate data sources into stable Oracle environments, ensuring high data integrity and providing the technical foundation for organizational decision-making.




Responsibilities

Data Engineering & ETL: Design, build, and optimize robust ETL pipelines from multiple source systems into Oracle databases. Perform complex data transformations to support quality assurance and asset inspection processes.
Technical Leadership: Act as a lead consultant, providing development oversight for Oracle database systems. Review functional specifications and present technical designs to development leads to ensure alignment with ’s architectural standards.
System Maintenance & Optimization: Develop, test, and maintain SQL, PowerShell, and VBA/VBScript solutions. Resolve usage issues, perform system integration activities, and execute batch processing to ensure 24/7 operational effectiveness.
Database Administration: Provide local DBA support, including managing access controls, user privileges, and ensuring the security of asset data.
Stakeholder Engagement: Lead requirements gathering sessions with business units, translate needs into conceptual design models, and facilitate User Acceptance Testing (UAT).
Documentation & Knowledge Transfer: Create detailed process flows, job aids, and standard operating procedures (SOPs) to build long-term capability and capacity within the organization.




Qualifications

9 years of consulting experience in reporting and analytics (or equivalent).
5 years (minimum 3 years with proven practice) of hands-on development in Oracle SQL, Oracle PL/SQL, and PowerShell.
Technical Skills
Database Expert: Advanced proficiency in Oracle SQL and PL/SQL; experience with performance tuning and designing stable, integrated data architectures.
Scripting & Automation: Strong PowerShell skills for process automation; additional experience in VBA/VBScript for legacy system support.
ETL/Data Modeling: Expert skills in data models and the construction of information architectures that enable integrated reporting.
Tooling: Familiarity with Web Intelligence, Dashboarding tools, and Analysis frameworks.
Problem Solving: A proactive approach to sourcing technical solutions (searching/integrating) rather than simple copy-pasting.

Professional Competencies

Analytical Thinking: Ability to translate complex business requirements into feasible technical options.
Change Management: Advanced knowledge of organizational change management procedures and policies.
Communication: Excellent written and verbal communication skills, capable of providing guidance to technical teams and status updates to leadership.

Education and Certifications

Bachelor’s degree or Technical Diploma in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
Nice to have Certifications (Oracle Database SQL Certified Associate, Oracle PL/SQL Developer Certified Professional, Microsoft Certified: Power Platform Functional Consultant Associate, ITIL 4 Foundation Ect)

Additional Information

Candidates in this role will be working a a Hybrid Schedule (Monday and Tuesday in office days and Wed-Friday remote)
Every contractor must supply their own Windows 11 Laptop computer for the duration of the assignment.
Every contractor must supply their own “Smart Phone”. This is needed to gain access to the Organizations network.

Title: Security Guard
Description:
Company Description


Cabal Security & Investigations Ltd., based in British Columbia, Canada, provides professional and reliable security solutions tailored for commercial, retail, and residential properties. Our team of experienced security personnel is trained to deliver high-quality services, including tactical uniformed guarding, mobile patrol, undercover loss prevention, and concierge services. With expertise in CCTV monitoring, incident response, access control, and collaboration with emergency services, we ensure a safe environment for our clients. As a member of the BC BOLO Group, we have advanced capabilities in identifying and addressing security risks proactively. Our mission is to provide dependable and cost-effective security services while setting the standard for quality and trust in the industry.


Role Description


This is a full-time, on-site Security Guard position located in Vancouver, BC. The Security Guard will be responsible for monitoring and patrolling assigned areas to prevent unauthorized entry, theft, and vandalism. This role involves ensuring the safety of people and property, responding to incidents, and coordinating with emergency services when required. Additional duties include managing access control, monitoring CCTV systems, performing site inspections, and providing excellent customer service to both clients and visitors.


Qualifications

Strong Interpersonal and Customer Service skills for engaging with the general public, clients, and team members effectively
Proficiency in Physical Security and Personnel Security methods, ensuring the safety of people and property
Experience in Private Security and knowledge of security protocols for commercial, retail, or residential environments
Ability to remain calm under pressure and make sound decisions in high-pressure situations
Minimum of two years of hands-on security experience
Valid British Columbia Security Worker License
First Aid/CPR certification is an asset
Strong observational and reporting skills
Ability to work flexible hours, including nights, weekends, and holidays
Physical fitness and ability to perform foot patrols and manage emergency situations

Title: Statistician
Description:
Responsible for the application of biostatistical methods to support clinical trials and data management, including statistical planning, design, analysis, and reporting throughout the project life cycle in accordance with project, organizational and regulatory standards.  Act as subject matter expert, providing statistical support to project teams, quality/study auditors, and external stakeholders. Participate in the promotion of Alimentiv as a global research organization, by contributing to the development, publishing, and reporting of project data. Please note we are hiring for multiple positions at this time.




Analysis Services


Carry out statistical analyses and provide direction to programmers, using standard software packages to interpret and translate results within a statistical framework into biomedical terms and prepare statistical data results for inclusion in reports and research papers that meet trial, organizational and regulatory best practices, and standards







Academic Research Support


Working in conjunction with the Director of Academic Research, convert and present new and novel biometrical trial technologies, processes and findings into publishable and promotable scientific processes and results







Project Support


Provide statistical expertise and guidance based on established methodologies throughout the project life cycle in the areas of study policies, protocols, implementation timelines and processes, data interpretation and publication of results


Work with data management team to ensure data captured incorporates and corresponds to project protocols and requirements.  







Statistical Analysis/Data Collection Planning


Prepare statistical analysis plan and oversee project data collection, management, and analysis with input from the project teams that incorporates the type of information collected, sample size, randomization procedures, table and listing shells, project statistical policies, protocols, and methodology to be used to appropriately translate scientific questions into statistical hypotheses







Study Report Support


Assist medical director(s) and/or technical writers by preparing statistical section of study report, publications and/or presentations ensuring statistical analyses adheres to trial, sponsor, organizational and regulatory requirements, and best practices







Qualifications


A minimum of a Bachelor's or Master's degree and a professional designation + 1-3 years of related experience


Experience in the industry, or in an academic setting is preferred


Very strong communication skills is a must




 




Working Conditions


Home-based










Stay Alert to Recruitment Scams
We have been made aware of individuals posing as our recruiters and contacting candidates with fraudulent opportunities. Our hiring process always includes direct engagement with a recruiter or hiring manager, and we will never ask for sensitive personal information early in the process. Official communication will only come from our company email domains. If something doesn’t feel right, we encourage you to verify before responding.




We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Business Insights Analyst - FTT
Description:
Marketing Statement

Join a company that has been a part of moving Metro Vancouver for over 35 years.

British Columbia Rapid Transit Company Ltd. (BCRTC) maintains and operates the Expo and Millennium SkyTrain Lines from our central facilities in south Burnaby. Our services connect downtown Vancouver to the cities of Burnaby, New Westminster, Coquitlam, Port Moody and Surrey. With the future expansion of both the Expo and Millennium Lines gathering pace, infrastructure renewal and new asset acquisition projects underway, this is an exciting time to join our team of over 1,200 dedicated employees, working on one of the longest fully-automated rapid transit systems in the world.

Our work encompasses roles such as administration, engineering, elevator and escalator maintenance, field operations, vehicle maintenance, and wayside (station and guideway) maintenance.

When you work for SkyTrain, not only will you help move Metro Vancouver, you will also have access to comprehensive benefits including paid vacation, medical benefits, transit passes, training and more. We are a proud equal opportunity employer, committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce.

Come explore what makes us one of BC’s Top Employers!

Responsibilities

PRIMARY PURPOSE

Develops, coordinates and maintains performance measures across the organization to ensure alignment with corporate

goals and objectives. Prepares a variety of analyses and reports to provide accurate and up-to-date information to the

management team.

Develops and maintains end-to-end data and analytics solutions to meet BCRTC’s business needs and support data-driven decision-making strategies. This includes identifying analytical goals and objectives through stakeholder engagement, developing data collection solutions, creating automated Extract-Transform-Load (ETL) processes, generating meaningful performance insights, and communicating analytical insights to various stakeholders through data visualization tools and data storytelling.

Key Accountabilities

Performance Measures & Continuous Improvement:

Collaborates with departments to explore data requirements and works with relevant managers/supervisors to develop business performance tracking and analytical solutions across all functions. Engages with relevant stakeholders in requirements gathering, data tracking, report design, delivery and sustainment.

Investigates and analyzes statistics and underlying causes and trends of business performance. Prepares periodic reports indicating business performance compared to targets and the previous year(s), and rationale for variances. Supports all corporate KPI reporting activities for various levels of management, BCRTC Board of Directors, and TransLink.

Establishes and maintains data collection, verification, and reporting processes which ensures timeliness, accuracy, and completeness of the analytical insights. Ensures solutions are reliable and comply with company’s standards, policies, processes, regulatory and other related requirements.

Leverages available benchmarking resources for best practices and provides comparable performance measures from other transit agencies and business enterprises to the concerned stakeholders towards informed decision making. Participates in annual benchmarking KPI data submission activities.

Supports corporate data & insights-driven initiatives and business planning activities as needed, e.g. Annual Performance Target Setting, Data Quality Improvement, Data Value Creation Initiatives and Data Management framework.

Supports the design, implementation, and maintenance of pipelines for data integration and ETL/ELT on modern data platforms such as Microsoft Azure Synapse, Fabric, and PowerBI.

Supports the manager to produce reports and statistical analyses for targeted areas in response to requests from media, Freedom of Information requests, Executive team, BCRTC management and supervisors.

Business Intelligence & Analytics (BI&A) Services

Provides analytical services as requested from leaders across the organization on divisional and departmental level BI&A reporting, business process digitization and automation. Ensures end users have the information needed to manage their business performance to support a culture of continuous improvement.

Designs, implements, and maintains end-to-end analytical solutions for BCRTC internal clients using modern BI platforms such as Microsoft Azure Synapse, Fabric, PowerApp, PowerAutomate, and Power BI. Ensures designed solutions are fit for purpose, cost-effective, align with standards, policies and support ongoing data & insights-driven initiatives within BCRTC. Ensures that solutions are reusable and scalable following best practices, balancing immediate short-term and long-term benefits.

Processes sensitive workforce data and develops relevant BI solutions according to enterprise policies and guidelines. Supports the completion of requisite Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA) completion for the developed data and analytics solutions.

Qualifications

EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE:

The requirements for this job are acquired through completion of a bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Data Management, Data Analytics, Engineering, or other relevant business-related fields plus four (4) years of experience with development and analysis of performance measurements, and technical experience with design, implementation and maintenance of data and creation of reports in a Business Intelligence environment. Master’s, Doctorate degrees, and Professional Certifications in related fields may be considered as equivalent to practical experience. Experience supporting data management or data governance programs, or in workforce-related analytics for occupational health, safety, workforce productivity and other HR analytics would be considered an asset.

Other Requirements

Solid understanding of concepts, principles, practices and techniques pertaining to all aspects of performance measures, benchmarking, balanced scorecards and dashboards.

Advanced knowledge of developing Microsoft Analytics solutions including PowerApps, Power Automate, Power BI, Azure Synapse and Fabric. Demonstrated experience in programming languages is required: e.g. SQL, R, Python.

Strong analytical, problem solving and judgment skills to be able to interpret data and diagnose data quality issues.

Strong time management, organization, verbal and written communication skills.

Strong relationship management skills and ability to work across multidisciplinary teams and management levels.

Ability to multi-task, manage multiple priorities and work in a rapidly changing and dynamic environment with limited supervision.

Ability to listen and interpret stakeholder feedback to understand business processes and identify business issues/objectives.

Other Information

Recruitment Process: An applicant will be required to demonstrate their suitability for this position by meeting the minimum level of qualifications and experience in order to be invited into the selection process. A standard interview format will be used including general, scenario and behavioural descriptive interview questions.

Work Schedule

37.5 hours per week.

This is a full-time temporary position.

Work Designation

Resident

This position works predominately on-site.

Rate of Pay

The salary for this position ranges from $77,520 - $116,280 per annum. Actual salary offered will be based on education, experience, skills, and qualifications as they relate to the role.

We aim to provide competitive pay that reflects your professional background and expertise. We want to ensure our offer will align with your strengths considering experience, skills, and internal equity to offer a fair and equitable salary. The Total Compensation Package, which includes Extended Health, Dental, Transit Pass and enrollment in the Public Service Pension Plan upon eligibility as well as other employment offerings will be discussed in detail as your application progresses.

How To Apply

Please click the 'Apply' button at the top right corner or go to http://www.translink.ca/careers to apply for this position and view instructions on the process.

INSTRUCTIONS: Please save your (1) cover letter, and your (2) resume as one pdf document prior to uploading your application on-line.

Posting Date: April 14 2026

Closing Date: Open Until Filled

Please note that only those short listed will be contacted.

Having trouble applying? Please view the System Requirements & FAQ's by going to http://www.translink.ca/careers.

Equal Employment Opportunity

SkyTrain is committed to employment equity and building a diverse workforce, representative of the customers we serve and the many communities in the Metro Vancouver region. We welcome and encourage Indigenous applicants, people of colour, all genders, 2SLGBTQ+ and persons with disabilities to apply. Learn more about TransLink's commitment to to equity, diversity and inclusion.

Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. For a confidential inquiry, simply email us at jobs@translink.ca.

Marketing Statement

Join a company that has been a part of moving Metro Vancouver for over 35 years.

British Columbia Rapid Transit Company Ltd. (BCRTC) maintains and operates the Expo and Millennium SkyTrain Lines from our central facilities in south Burnaby. Our services connect downtown Vancouver to the cities of Burnaby, New Westminster, Coquitlam, Port Moody and Surrey. With the future expansion of both the Expo and Millennium Lines gathering pace, infrastructure renewal and new asset acquisition projects underway, this is an exciting time to join our team of over 1,200 dedicated employees, working on one of the longest fully-automated rapid transit systems in the world.

Our work encompasses roles such as administration, engineering, elevator and escalator maintenance, field operations, vehicle maintenance, and wayside (station and guideway) maintenance.

When you work for SkyTrain, not only will you help move Metro Vancouver, you will also have access to comprehensive benefits including paid vacation, medical benefits, transit passes, training and more. We are a proud equal opportunity employer, committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce.

Come explore what makes us one of BC’s Top Employers!

Responsibilities

PRIMARY PURPOSE

Develops, coordinates and maintains performance measures across the organization to ensure alignment with corporate

goals and objectives. Prepares a variety of analyses and reports to provide accurate and up-to-date information to the

management team.

Develops and maintains end-to-end data and analytics solutions to meet BCRTC’s business needs and support data-driven decision-making strategies. This includes identifying analytical goals and objectives through stakeholder engagement, developing data collection solutions, creating automated Extract-Transform-Load (ETL) processes, generating meaningful performance insights, and communicating analytical insights to various stakeholders through data visualization tools and data storytelling.

Key Accountabilities

Performance Measures & Continuous Improvement:

Collaborates with departments to explore data requirements and works with relevant managers/supervisors to develop business performance tracking and analytical solutions across all functions. Engages with relevant stakeholders in requirements gathering, data tracking, report design, delivery and sustainment.

Investigates and analyzes statistics and underlying causes and trends of business performance. Prepares periodic reports indicating business performance compared to targets and the previous year(s), and rationale for variances. Supports all corporate KPI reporting activities for various levels of management, BCRTC Board of Directors, and TransLink.

Establishes and maintains data collection, verification, and reporting processes which ensures timeliness, accuracy, and completeness of the analytical insights. Ensures solutions are reliable and comply with company’s standards, policies, processes, regulatory and other related requirements.

Leverages available benchmarking resources for best practices and provides comparable performance measures from other transit agencies and business enterprises to the concerned stakeholders towards informed decision making. Participates in annual benchmarking KPI data submission activities.

Supports corporate data & insights-driven initiatives and business planning activities as needed, e.g. Annual Performance Target Setting, Data Quality Improvement, Data Value Creation Initiatives and Data Management framework.

Supports the design, implementation, and maintenance of pipelines for data integration and ETL/ELT on modern data platforms such as Microsoft Azure Synapse, Fabric, and PowerBI.

Supports the manager to produce reports and statistical analyses for targeted areas in response to requests from media, Freedom of Information requests, Executive team, BCRTC management and supervisors.

Business Intelligence & Analytics (BI&A) Services

Provides analytical services as requested from leaders across the organization on divisional and departmental level BI&A reporting, business process digitization and automation. Ensures end users have the information needed to manage their business performance to support a culture of continuous improvement.

Designs, implements, and maintains end-to-end analytical solutions for BCRTC internal clients using modern BI platforms such as Microsoft Azure Synapse, Fabric, PowerApp, PowerAutomate, and Power BI. Ensures designed solutions are fit for purpose, cost-effective, align with standards, policies and support ongoing data & insights-driven initiatives within BCRTC. Ensures that solutions are reusable and scalable following best practices, balancing immediate short-term and long-term benefits.

Processes sensitive workforce data and develops relevant BI solutions according to enterprise policies and guidelines. Supports the completion of requisite Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA) completion for the developed data and analytics solutions.

Qualifications

EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE:

The requirements for this job are acquired through completion of a bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Data Management, Data Analytics, Engineering, or other relevant business-related fields plus four (4) years of experience with development and analysis of performance measurements, and technical experience with design, implementation and maintenance of data and creation of reports in a Business Intelligence environment. Master’s, Doctorate degrees, and Professional Certifications in related fields may be considered as equivalent to practical experience. Experience supporting data management or data governance programs, or in workforce-related analytics for occupational health, safety, workforce productivity and other HR analytics would be considered an asset.

Other Requirements

Solid understanding of concepts, principles, practices and techniques pertaining to all aspects of performance measures, benchmarking, balanced scorecards and dashboards.

Advanced knowledge of developing Microsoft Analytics solutions including PowerApps, Power Automate, Power BI, Azure Synapse and Fabric. Demonstrated experience in programming languages is required: e.g. SQL, R, Python.

Strong analytical, problem solving and judgment skills to be able to interpret data and diagnose data quality issues.

Strong time management, organization, verbal and written communication skills.

Strong relationship management skills and ability to work across multidisciplinary teams and management levels.

Ability to multi-task, manage multiple priorities and work in a rapidly changing and dynamic environment with limited supervision.

Ability to listen and interpret stakeholder feedback to understand business processes and identify business issues/objectives.

Other Information

Recruitment Process: An applicant will be required to demonstrate their suitability for this position by meeting the minimum level of qualifications and experience in order to be invited into the selection process. A standard interview format will be used including general, scenario and behavioural descriptive interview questions.

Work Schedule

37.5 hours per week.

This is a full-time temporary position.

Work Designation

Resident

This position works predominately on-site.

Rate of Pay

The salary for this position ranges from $77,520 - $116,280 per annum. Actual salary offered will be based on education, experience, skills, and qualifications as they relate to the role.

We aim to provide competitive pay that reflects your professional background and expertise. We want to ensure our offer will align with your strengths considering experience, skills, and internal equity to offer a fair and equitable salary. The Total Compensation Package, which includes Extended Health, Dental, Transit Pass and enrollment in the Public Service Pension Plan upon eligibility as well as other employment offerings will be discussed in detail as your application progresses.

How To Apply

Please click the 'Apply' button at the top right corner or go to http://www.translink.ca/careers to apply for this position and view instructions on the process.

INSTRUCTIONS: Please save your (1) cover letter, and your (2) resume as one pdf document prior to uploading your application on-line.

Posting Date: April 14 2026

Closing Date: Open Until Filled

Please note that only those short listed will be contacted.

Having trouble applying? Please view the System Requirements & FAQ's by going to http://www.translink.ca/careers.

Equal Employment Opportunity

SkyTrain is committed to employment equity and building a diverse workforce, representative of the customers we serve and the many communities in the Metro Vancouver region. We welcome and encourage Indigenous applicants, people of colour, all genders, 2SLGBTQ+ and persons with disabilities to apply. Learn more about TransLink's commitment to to equity, diversity and inclusion.

Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. For a confidential inquiry, simply email us at jobs@translink.ca.

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
About Dialpad

Dialpad is the AI-native business communications platform. We unify calling, messaging, meetings, and contact center on a single platform - powered by AI that understands every conversation in real time.

More than 70,000 companies around the globe, including WeWork, Asana, NASDAQ, AAA Insurance, COMPASS Realty, Uber, Randstad, and Tractor Supply, rely on Dialpad to build stronger customer connections using real-time, AI-driven insights.

We’re now leading the shift to Agentic AI: intelligent agents that don’t just analyze conversations but take action by automating workflows, resolving customer issues, and accelerating revenue in real time. Our DAART initiative (Dialpad Agentic AI in Real Time) is redefining what a communications platform can do.

Visit dialpad.com to learn more.

Being a Dialer

At Dialpad, AI isn’t just a feature; it’s how our teams do their best work every day. We put powerful AI tools in every employee’s hands so they can move faster, think bigger, and achieve more.

We believe every conversation matters. And we’ve built the platform that turns those conversations into insight and action, for our customers and ourselves.

We look for people who are intensely curious and hold themselves to a high bar. Our ambition is significant, and achieving it requires a team that operates at the highest level. We seek individuals who embody our core traits: Scrappy, Curious, Optimistic, Persistent, and Empathetic.

Your role

As an analytics engineer, you’ll be an integral part of our data analysis and QA team, providing essential data support for our cutting-edge Agentic AI initiatives, specifically with ASR and NLP teams. A key focus will be on leveraging data analysis to identify and drive optimization of the conversational flows and underlying data infrastructure for agentic voice and chat solutions. You will actively seek opportunities to improve the team’s productivity and workflow through automation and process optimization, including implementing automated QA for our agentic systems.

This position reports to the manager of the Data Analysis and QA team and has the opportunity to be based in our Vancouver, Canada Office.

What You’ll Do


You will work closely with other Agentic AI teams to test and evaluate our Agentic voice and chat solutions.
You will design and implement data logging and monitoring strategies to capture key analytical insights.
You will utilize data analysis to identify areas for optimization in the voice bot's conversational flows and data pipeline.
You will build and maintain data pipelines on Vertex AI pipelines.
You will work closely with the AI Platform team to build tooling for data science projects.
You will implement automation and processes to improve our workflow.
You will create and maintain dashboards (Tableau) and data pipelines (Dataform) that help drive product and business decisions.
You will contribute to our continuous efforts to enforce data privacy and compliance.
You will collaborate with cross-functional teams, including AI, engineering, and product teams.


Skills You’ll Bring


Bachelor's or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related fields.
1 - 3 years of working experience with software engineering or data engineering projects.
1 - 3 years of experience with Python, working with GCP, including storage, BigQuery, Compute, Kubernetes, or similar.
1 - 3 years of experience with SQL, able to optimize complex SQL queries and build data pipelines.
Experience with BI tools such as Tableau.
Experience analyzing the performance of conversational AI systems (e.g., voice bots, chatbots) and collaborating with cross-functional teams (AI, Product) on data-driven improvements.
Experience working with popular LLM frameworks.
Strong problem-solving and analytical abilities, with the capacity to handle complex technical and analytical problems.


For exceptional talent based in British Columbia, Canada the target base salary range for this position is posted below. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the target range for new hire salaries for the position. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process. Please note that the compensation details listed in British Columbia role postings reflect the base salary only, and do not include bonus, equity, or benefits.

British Columbia, Canada Salary Range

$109,500—$138,750 CAD

Why Join Dialpad


Work at the center of the AI transformation in business communications
Build and ship agentic AI products that are redefining how companies operate
Join a team where AI amplifies every employee’s impact
Competitive salary, comprehensive benefits, and real opportunities for growth


We believe in investing in our people. Dialpad offers competitive benefits and perks, cutting-edge AI tools, and a robust training program that help you reach your full potential. We have designed our offices to be inclusive, offering a vibrant environment to cultivate collaboration and connection. Our exceptional culture, repeatedly recognized as a Great Place to Work, ensures that every employee feels valued and empowered to contribute to our collective success.

Don’t meet every single requirement? If you’re excited about this role and possess the fundamental traits, drive, and strong ambition we seek, but your experience doesn’t meet every qualification, we encourage you to apply.

Dialpad is an equal-opportunity employer. We are dedicated to creating a community of inclusion and an environment free from discrimination or harassment.

Title: GIS Analyst
Description:
Summary

As a GIS Analyst at Stantec, you’ll play a key role in delivering geospatial services and solutions to both internal project teams and external clients. Your work will primarily support projects in the Mining, Hydropower & Dams, Power Delivery, and Oil & Gas sectors. You’ll collaborate closely with stakeholders to understand their objectives and recommend the most effective GIS technologies and strategies to meet their business needs, bringing spatial insight to complex challenges.

Opportunity

We’re offering a dynamic full-time opportunity within our Energy & Resources business unit at Stantec, open to candidates-based Canada. While the role is location-flexible—with options for in-office, hybrid, or fully remote arrangements—preference may be given to applicants near one of our key target Canada hub offices:


Canada: Burnaby, BC, Vancouver, BC, Calgary, AB; Edmonton, AB, Markham, ON; Toronto, ON; Sudbury, ON; Fredericton, NB


You’ll be joining a vibrant, collaborative team of 12 GIS professionals supporting high-impact projects across North America and beyond. We’re looking for an intermediate, well-rounded, driven GIS Analyst with strong experience in desktop GIS (ArcGIS Pro), ArcGIS Online, and mobile GIS platforms.

Key Responsibilities


Collaborate with multidisciplinary project teams to design and implement geospatial strategies that align with client goals—accurately scoping effort and delivering high-quality results from concept to completion
Serve as the lead GIS professional on small to mid-sized projects, managing scope, schedule, budget, and with potential to coordinate supporting GIS resources
Be an active participant and technical contributor on large, multi-year projects
Approach every task as an opportunity to innovate—rethinking how spatial data is collected, managed, visualized, and shared
Create clear, compelling cartographic products—both static and interactive (web)—with a strong sense of visual design and storytelling
Develop and manage dynamic GIS solutions using Esri products and applications such as Dashboards, Experience Builder, Hub Sites, StoryMaps, Field Maps, and Survey123.
Balance creative problem-solving with adherence to Stantec’s GIS standards and best practices
Provide mentorship and guidance to GIS team members across experience levels, fostering growth and collaboration regardless of staff seniority
Maintain a strong commitment to quality assurance, ensuring accuracy and consistency in all deliverables
Stay actively engaged with the GIS team and broader geospatial community, keeping pace with emerging technologies, tools, and industry trends
Demonstrate flexibility in adapting to changes in project scope, timelines, and budget—including occasional non-traditional work hours
Uphold a high standard of personal and team safety in all work environments


Capabilities And Credentials


Deep understanding and application of core GIS principles, spatial analysis, and geospatial workflows
Strong grasp of coordinate systems, projections, and data transformations
Skilled in acquiring, managing and analyzing both vector and raster datasets
Excellent cartographic design abilities for desktop and web-based mapping products
Experience with GIS-CAD interoperability and data integration
Proficient in Esri ArcGIS Pro for intermediate to advanced desktop GIS tasks
Intermediate to advanced expertise in ArcGIS Online and associated applications including Dashboards, Experience Builder, Hub Sites, and StoryMaps
Competent in intermediate to advanced mobile data collection workflows using Field Maps and Survey123
Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines
Effective communicator—able to convey technical concepts clearly across diverse audiences, both written and verbal
Highly motivated, adaptable, and collaborative—thrives in dynamic, multidisciplinary team environments
Committed to continuous learning and professional growth in both technical and consulting domains


Education And Experience


Bachelor’s degree or higher in GIS, geology, geomatics, geotechnical engineering, environmental science, or a related discipline, with proven GIS-focused coursework or training.
Minimum of five years of professional experience as a GIS Specialist or Analyst.


Additional Preferred Capabilities And Experience


Experience working in an Architecture, Engineering, and Construction (AEC) consulting environment is highly valued.
Experience applying GIS and Geospatial technologies within the Mining, Hydropower & Dams, Power Delivery, or Oil & Gas sectors.
Applied experience in remote sensing, raster processing and analysis.
GeoAI applied experience with ESRI’s deep learning models.
Bi-lingual in French and/or Spanish, in addition to English.


Note


The above job description is intended to represent only the key areas of responsibility; specific position assignments will vary depending on the business needs of the department.
Candidates may be asked to supply a portfolio of their work.


Pay Range


Locations in Lower Mainland - BC, GTA & Ottawa Ontario-$86,900.00 - $126,000.00 Annually


Pay Transparency: In compliance with pay transparency laws, pay ranges are provided for positions in locations where required. Please note, the final agreed upon compensation is based on individual education, qualifications, experience, and work location. At Stantec certain roles are bonus eligible. Actual compensation for part-time roles will be pro-rated based on the agreed number of working hours per week.

Benefits Summary: Regular full-time and part-time employees (working at least 20 hours per week) will have access to health, dental, and vision plans, a wellness program, health care spending account, wellness spending account, group registered retirement savings plan, employee stock purchase program, group tax-free savings account, life and accidental death & dismemberment (AD&D) insurance, short-term/long-term disability plans, emergency travel benefits, tuition reimbursement, professional membership fee coverage, and paid time off.

Temporary/casual employees will have access to group registered retirement savings plan, employee stock purchase program, and group tax-free savings account.

The benefits information listed above may not apply to union positions because benefits for such positions are governed by applicable collective bargaining agreements.
